$description: The canonical vocabulary of responsive reflow patterns — the ways an spec restructures as available width changes. Spec API contracts reference a pattern id in `responsive.reflow[].pattern` instead of re-describing the behavior in prose, the same way behaviors are captured as traits. Breakpoints where a pattern kicks in are referenced by id from tokens.json `breakpoint.*`. Consumed by the website, the conformance gate, and the Agent Skill.

fluid.label: Fluid
fluid.description: Fills the width of its container and grows/shrinks continuously; no structural change.

wrap.label: Wrap
wrap.description: A row of items wraps onto additional lines as width decreases, rather than overflowing or shrinking each item.

stack.label: Stack
stack.description: A horizontal arrangement becomes a vertical stack below the breakpoint.

reflow-columns.label: Reflow columns
reflow-columns.description: A multi-column grid steps its column count down as width decreases (e.g. 3 → 2 → 1).

collapse-to-menu.label: Collapse to menu
collapse-to-menu.description: Lower-priority items collapse behind a disclosure or overflow control (hamburger, ‘More’ menu) when they no longer fit.

drawer.label: Drawer
drawer.description: A persistent side region (sidebar, nav) becomes a toggled, off-canvas drawer over the content.

to-sheet.label: To sheet
to-sheet.description: A centered or anchored overlay becomes a full-width edge sheet (typically bottom) on small screens for reachability.

horizontal-scroll.label: Horizontal scroll
horizontal-scroll.description: Content that can't compress overflows into a horizontal scroller with clear affordance, instead of clipping.

reflow-to-cards.label: Reflow to cards
reflow-to-cards.description: Dense tabular rows restructure into a stack of label/value cards below the breakpoint.

reposition.label: Reposition
reposition.description: An anchored surface flips or shifts its placement to stay within the viewport as space runs out.

truncate.label: Truncate
truncate.description: Text or labels ellipsize (or drop to an icon) rather than wrap or push the layout.

hide-secondary.label: Hide secondary
hide-secondary.description: Non-essential elements are hidden below the breakpoint, keeping the essential content legible.
